title: Karma + no-tomorrow micro-creed, Mar 2024
html:
TL;DR

  • Two-line micro-creed:
    1. "What you put into the wrld comes back to you, and often in multiplying ways."
    2. "There is no tomorrow. Those that go after it are deserving. Death is imminent, so don't think you have time to waste."
  • A karma + memento mori pairing — Yahir compresses two whole moral systems into a tweet-length entry.

Key quotes

"What you put into the wrld comes back to you, and often in multiplying ways."

"Death is imminent, so don't think you have time to waste."

Why it matters in the graph

  • The "multiplying karma" line is restated almost verbatim in 2024 09 12 never forget you can do whatever the fuck you want ("the energy you put out into the universe comes back, often in multiplicative ways") — six months later. Same idea, more flourish.
  • Death-imminence is the operational form of the legacy question concept — already a tracked theme.
  • Pairs with the "if I were to die tomorrow" decision-frame in 2024 09 12 never forget you can do whatever the fuck you want.
  • The wrld brand suffix also appears here ("the wrld") — confirms it's a stylistic constant, not just a product naming choice.

Open questions

  • What event prompted this Mar 30 entry? It reads like post-something — a near-miss, a death in extended family, a milestone?

Wikilinks

legacy question · wrld brand suffix · 2024 09 12 never forget you can do whatever the fuck you want · fear of not doing
